registryName: VANTARIS ONE Legacy Ownership Migration Register
migrationPrinciples: Ownership migration starts with contracts, not table movement.
Physical storage does not determine semantic ownership.
Legacy routes remain behind compatibility façades until target parity.
No direct cross-module database access may be introduced.
New owners expose APIs/events before consumers migrate.
Dual write is prohibited by default.
Dual write requires explicit durable reconciliation and rollback design.
Read façades may be used temporarily when ownership is clear.
Legacy data must not be deleted before reconciliation and rollback proof.
Canonical identifiers must be preserved or explicitly mapped.
Audit records remain immutable during migration.
Evidence integrity and provenance must be preserved.
Package disablement must not orphan canonical records.
UFMS remains an external product; no UFMS data tables are absorbed into ONE without an approved contract.
Exception retirement requires validation, parity and rollback gates.

ownershipConflicts.title: Legacy DID identity, permission and trust persistence overlap

sourceEvidence.evidence: DID models persist identity, permission, relationship, VC anchors and revocations.

ownershipConflicts.targetSystemOfRecord: Governance & Security identity/permission services and Trust hash-anchor boundary
ownershipConflicts.readMigration: Preserve DID route compatibility behind Governance & Security providers.
ownershipConflicts.writeMigration: New identity/security writes use governed providers; trust anchors remain Trust-owned.
ownershipConflicts.identifierMapping: Preserve DID and VC IDs as source identifiers mapped to governed global IDs.
ownershipConflicts.auditMigration: Security administration and trust actions require fail-closed audit.
ownershipConflicts.evidenceMigration: HashAnchor may support EvidenceReference but remains distinct from EvidenceRecord.
ownershipConflicts.retentionMigration: Identity and trust records retain policy-controlled security retention.
ownershipConflicts.packageImpact: Consolidates legacy DID behavior under PKG-GOVERNANCE-SECURITY boundaries.
ownershipConflicts.deploymentImpact: Later provider migration; no identity runtime change here.
explicitNonScope: No schema, route, provider, model, import, adapter, or runtime change
ownershipConflicts.rollbackPlan: Preserve immutable legacy source and bounded provider-specific compatibility path until rollback window and approval close.
notes: Credential values are never migration metadata; only classified references and mappings.

ownershipConflicts.title: EDGE runtime versus canonical Device and Point boundary

sourceEvidence.evidence: Read-only gateway and connector setup/diagnostic placeholder.

ownershipConflicts.currentOwner: EDGE runtime placeholder and legacy IoT model
ownershipConflicts.targetSystemOfRecord: EDGE runtime plus Asset Graph canonical identity
ownershipConflicts.readMigration: EDGE resolves canonical Device/Point references from Asset Graph.
ownershipConflicts.writeMigration: EDGE writes runtime/discovery/mapping state only and proposes canonical changes through contracts.
ownershipConflicts.identifierMapping: Gateway/connector IDs remain EDGE IDs; Device/Point IDs remain Asset Graph IDs.
ownershipConflicts.auditMigration: Audit mapping approval, runtime controls and canonical link changes.
ownershipConflicts.evidenceMigration: Discovery evidence may reference observations without making EDGE EvidenceRecord owner.
ownershipConflicts.retentionMigration: Retain mapping source data through reconciliation.
ownershipConflicts.deploymentImpact: Requires explicit providers and adapters in later tasks.
explicitNonScope: No schema, route, provider, model, import, adapter, or runtime change
ownershipConflicts.rollbackPlan: Preserve immutable legacy source and bounded provider-specific compatibility path until rollback window and approval close.
notes: EDGE does not retain canonical Device or Point ownership.

ownershipConflicts.title: Legacy IoT mapping tables combine connector and asset concerns

sourceEvidence.evidence: Field/method mappings bind device DID, protocol, raw paths and canonical semantics.

ownershipConflicts.canonicalOwner: AN_VANTARIS_EDGE / Unified Data Model / ONE Asset Graph
ownershipConflicts.targetSystemOfRecord: EDGE mapping execution; Unified Data Model semantics; Asset Graph Point records
ownershipConflicts.readMigration: Expose mappings through Integration Governance/EDGE while consumers adopt canonical Point IDs.
ownershipConflicts.writeMigration: Mapping execution writes stay in EDGE; approved canonical Point changes go to Asset Graph.
ownershipConflicts.identifierMapping: Map device DID, raw field/path and standard semantic identifiers deterministically.
ownershipConflicts.auditMigration: Audit mapping approval and execution controls.
ownershipConflicts.evidenceMigration: Mapping evidence retains source observation references.
ownershipConflicts.retentionMigration: Retain legacy mappings until all active connector mappings reconcile.
ownershipConflicts.packageImpact: Clarifies PKG-EDGE, PKG-UNIFIED-DATA-MODEL and PKG-ASSET-GRAPH dependencies.
ownershipConflicts.deploymentImpact: Requires bounded provider-specific mapping adapter, never generic one-adapter.
explicitNonScope: No schema, route, provider, model, import, adapter, or runtime change
ownershipConflicts.rollbackPlan: Preserve immutable legacy source and bounded provider-specific compatibility path until rollback window and approval close.
notes: No direct cross-module database access may be introduced.

ownershipConflicts.title: Legacy Device, Point and connector configuration ownership split

sourceEvidence.evidence: imbs_device combines device identity, status, protocol, connection configuration and credential material.

ownershipConflicts.canonicalOwner: ONE Asset Graph / AN_VANTARIS_EDGE / Governance & Security
ownershipConflicts.targetSystemOfRecord: ONE Asset Graph for Device/Point/Tag; EDGE for runtime connectivity; Governance & Security for credentials
ownershipConflicts.readMigration: Expose canonical Device/Point reads through Asset Graph while preserving bounded legacy reads.
ownershipConflicts.writeMigration: Target-first write to Asset Graph and EDGE-specific configuration APIs after parity; legacy writes then disabled.
ownershipConflicts.identifierMapping: Preserve device DID/code as source identifiers and map to UUID global IDs.
ownershipConflicts.auditMigration: Audit every identifier mapping, credential separation and write cutover.
ownershipConflicts.evidenceMigration: Evidence references retain source record ID and migration digest where required.
ownershipConflicts.retentionMigration: Legacy source retained through reconciliation and rollback window.
ownershipConflicts.packageImpact: PKG-ASSET-GRAPH and PKG-EDGE providers must be independently deployable.
ownershipConflicts.deploymentImpact: Requires provider additions and later bounded compatibility façade; no runtime change in this task.
explicitNonScope: No schema, route, provider, model, import, adapter, or runtime change
ownershipConflicts.rollbackPlan: Preserve immutable legacy source and bounded provider-specific compatibility path until rollback window and approval close.
notes: EDGE owns runtime, discovery and mapping execution; it does not own canonical Device or Point.

ownershipConflicts.title: Local modeling CSV persistence mixes AI execution and model metadata

sourceEvidence.evidence: Per-device local CSV files support training and prediction data.

ownershipConflicts.targetSystemOfRecord: Nexus AI for request traces; AI Model Hub for model metadata/version lifecycle
ownershipConflicts.readMigration: Read local model information through bounded compatibility while target registries are built.
ownershipConflicts.writeMigration: AI requests write trace to Nexus AI; model registration/deployment writes to AI Model Hub.
ownershipConflicts.identifierMapping: Map device_code dataset references separately from model IDs and versions.
ownershipConflicts.auditMigration: AI requests, reviews and model lifecycle actions remain auditable.
ownershipConflicts.evidenceMigration: Training provenance may create EvidenceReference without making local CSV an EvidenceRecord store.
ownershipConflicts.retentionMigration: Retain source datasets according to approved model/training retention.
ownershipConflicts.packageImpact: Splits PKG-NEXUS-AI execution from PKG-AI-MODEL-HUB lifecycle.
ownershipConflicts.deploymentImpact: Target providers precede retirement of local CSV paths.
explicitNonScope: No schema, route, provider, model, import, adapter, or runtime change
ownershipConflicts.rollbackPlan: Preserve immutable legacy source and bounded provider-specific compatibility path until rollback window and approval close.
notes: No physical model registry table is invented.

ownershipConflicts.title: Reports local JSONL audit persistence
ownershipConflicts.readMigration: Reports consumes central audit query/export APIs; JSONL remains bounded read compatibility.
ownershipConflicts.writeMigration: Reports emits AuditRecord through Governance & Security; no local authoritative writes after cutover.
ownershipConflicts.identifierMapping: Preserve auditId and hash-chain digests as source lineage references.
ownershipConflicts.auditMigration: Target AuditRecord emission is authoritative; local records are immutable migration inputs.
ownershipConflicts.evidenceMigration: EvidenceReference may link migrated audit IDs without converting AuditRecord into EvidenceRecord.
ownershipConflicts.retentionMigration: Retain JSONL immutably until central counts, IDs and digests reconcile.
ownershipConflicts.packageImpact: PKG-REPORTS becomes consumer; PKG-GOVERNANCE-SECURITY becomes provider.
ownershipConflicts.deploymentImpact: Central audit provider precedes Reports read migration.
explicitNonScope: No schema, route, provider, model, import, adapter, or runtime change
ownershipConflicts.rollbackPlan: Preserve immutable legacy source and bounded provider-specific compatibility path until rollback window and approval close.
notes: Reports JSONL is never the target AuditRecord system of record.

ownershipConflicts.title: UCDE local EvidenceRecord and integrity skeleton

sourceEvidence.evidence: Local mock EvidenceRecord, relationships and hash verification metadata.

ownershipConflicts.targetSystemOfRecord: Evidence Center and Governance & Security / Trust
ownershipConflicts.readMigration: UCDE reads Evidence Center records and workspace projections.
ownershipConflicts.writeMigration: Evidence creation, relationships, provenance, integrity and retention write through Evidence Center.
ownershipConflicts.identifierMapping: Preserve evidence IDs, source references, relationship IDs and hash anchors.
ownershipConflicts.auditMigration: Evidence changes and verification actions emit authoritative AuditRecord.
ownershipConflicts.evidenceMigration: Preserve provenance, relationships, digests and HashAnchor trust references.
ownershipConflicts.retentionMigration: No evidence source removed until provenance and digest parity pass.
ownershipConflicts.packageImpact: PKG-UCDE becomes workspace consumer of PKG-EVIDENCE-CENTER.
ownershipConflicts.deploymentImpact: Evidence Center provider precedes UCDE consumer migration.
explicitNonScope: No schema, route, provider, model, import, adapter, or runtime change
ownershipConflicts.rollbackPlan: Preserve immutable legacy source and bounded provider-specific compatibility path until rollback window and approval close.
notes: UCDE retains investigation UI/workspace only and does not retain EvidenceRecord ownership.

ownershipConflicts.title: UCore classification and context overlap with asset semantics

sourceEvidence.evidence: UCore/system model duplicates entity classification and imports IoT field/method models.

ownershipConflicts.canonicalOwner: UCore / Unified Data Model / ONE Asset Graph
ownershipConflicts.targetSystemOfRecord: UCore for operational context; Unified Data Model for schema; Asset Graph for assets
ownershipConflicts.readMigration: Retain system route compatibility while reads are redirected to explicit owners.
ownershipConflicts.writeMigration: UCore writes operational context only; asset/schema writes go to canonical owners.
ownershipConflicts.identifierMapping: Map legacy entity type and menu context IDs without treating them as Asset IDs.
ownershipConflicts.auditMigration: Audit owner selection and write routing.
ownershipConflicts.evidenceMigration: Not applicable except references to governed owner records.
ownershipConflicts.retentionMigration: Preserve system configuration through cutover and rollback.
ownershipConflicts.packageImpact: Clarifies dependencies among PKG-UCORE, PKG-UNIFIED-DATA-MODEL and PKG-ASSET-GRAPH.
ownershipConflicts.deploymentImpact: Contract and route-provider changes in later tasks.
explicitNonScope: No schema, route, provider, model, import, adapter, or runtime change
ownershipConflicts.rollbackPlan: Preserve immutable legacy source and bounded provider-specific compatibility path until rollback window and approval close.
notes: UCore must not become Site/Building/Floor/Zone/Space/System/Asset/Equipment system of record.

ownershipConflicts.title: UFMS external integration placeholder boundary

sourceEvidence.evidence: Frozen external integration namespace without local UFMS runtime or data store.

ownershipConflicts.readMigration: Use approved external contract projections only after live interface verification.
ownershipConflicts.writeMigration: No internal UFMS placeholder may become an authoritative writer.
ownershipConflicts.identifierMapping: External UFMS IDs map to contract-governed source IDs; no UFMS table is absorbed.
ownershipConflicts.auditMigration: Adapter intake, fault actions and WorkOrderIntent require authoritative audit.
ownershipConflicts.evidenceMigration: EvidenceReference may link UFMS source records without copying confidential payloads.
ownershipConflicts.retentionMigration: External retention remains UFMS-owned; ONE retains only contract-approved records.
ownershipConflicts.packageImpact: PKG-UFMS-INTEGRATION remains an external-product integration package.
ownershipConflicts.deploymentImpact: Implementation blocked on live UFMS details.
explicitNonScope: No schema, route, provider, model, import, adapter, or runtime change
ownershipConflicts.rollbackPlan: Preserve immutable legacy source and bounded provider-specific compatibility path until rollback window and approval close.
notes: No live UFMS endpoint or source verification was performed.

ownershipConflicts.title: UMMS generic WorkOrder skeleton ownership

sourceEvidence.evidence: Read-only mock generic work-order lifecycle records.

ownershipConflicts.targetSystemOfRecord: ONE Work Management for generic lifecycle; UMMS for maintenance extensions
ownershipConflicts.readMigration: UMMS reads canonical WorkOrder and joins maintenance extension data.
ownershipConflicts.writeMigration: All generic lifecycle writes go to Work Management; UMMS writes only maintenance-owned extensions.
ownershipConflicts.identifierMapping: Preserve work order source IDs and map extension foreign references to canonical WorkOrder IDs.
ownershipConflicts.auditMigration: Audit lifecycle transitions at Work Management and extension changes at UMMS.
ownershipConflicts.evidenceMigration: Maintenance evidence references canonical WorkOrder and owning audit records.
ownershipConflicts.retentionMigration: Mock source retained until provider parity; no production data deletion implied.
ownershipConflicts.packageImpact: Adds PKG-WORK-MANAGEMENT provider dependency to UMMS.
ownershipConflicts.deploymentImpact: Work provider foundation precedes UMMS consumer migration.
explicitNonScope: No schema, route, provider, model, import, adapter, or runtime change
ownershipConflicts.rollbackPlan: Preserve immutable legacy source and bounded provider-specific compatibility path until rollback window and approval close.
notes: UMMS does not own assignment, dispatch, execution, closure, SLA or generic WorkOrder lifecycle.

migrationWaves.objective: Freeze objects, IDs, APIs, events and ownership; no data movement.
rollbackRequirements: No runtime rollback needed; preserve baseline

migrationWaves.objective: Create target owner service/provider while retaining legacy read compatibility.
rollbackRequirements: Disable new provider and retain legacy path

migrationWaves.objective: Consumers read through target owner or bounded compatibility façade.
rollbackRequirements: Revert consumers to bounded legacy reads
exitCriteria: All consumers read target or façade

migrationWaves.objective: Route new writes to target owner after parity.
rollbackRequirements: Restore legacy-first compatibility within bounded window

migrationWaves.objective: Compare counts, IDs, digests, status and relationships and resolve drift.
rollbackRequirements: Keep legacy immutable and revert cutover if thresholds fail

migrationWaves.objective: Retire compatibility only after rollback window and approval.

compatibilityPolicies.purpose: Temporary canonical reads over legacy source
Preserve a specific legacy route while target provider owns behavior
Translate a specific legacy DTO to/from target contract
Map source identifiers to canonical IDs
Build a consumer projection from canonical owner APIs/events
Preserve legacy export/read format during migration
Prohibit internal emulation of an external or security-owned system

reconciliationPolicies.comparisonMethod: Verify evidence source and relationship provenance

rollbackPolicies.trigger: Critical reconciliation, integrity, authorization, or availability failure

exceptionRetirementPlan.currentReason: Legacy Device model combines canonical identity, runtime connectivity and credential concerns.
Reports local JSONL predates central AuditRecord provider.
UMMS read-only mock predates Work Management.
exceptionRetirementPlan.targetParityGate: Work lifecycle and UMMS extension parity

exceptionRetirementPlan.currentReason: UCDE mock evidence predates Evidence Center.
exceptionRetirementPlan.targetParityGate: Evidence records, relationships and integrity parity
